name: Della H. Raney
summary: Della Hayden Raney was an American nurse in the Army Nurse Corps. Raney was the first African American nurse to report for duty in World War II and the first to be appointed chief nurse. In 1944, she became the first black nurse affiliated with the Army Air Corps promoted to captain, and she was later promoted to major in 1946. Raney retired from the Army in 1978.
original_caption: Capt. Della H. Raney, Army Nurse Corps, head of nursing staff at station hospital at Camp Beale, CA

wikipedia_summary.title: Della H. Raney
wikipedia_summary.extract: Della Hayden Raney was an American nurse in the Army Nurse Corps. Raney was the first African American nurse to report for duty in World War II and the first to be appointed chief nurse. In 1944, she became the first black nurse affiliated with the Army Air Corps promoted to captain, and she was later promoted to major in 1946. Raney retired from the Army in 1978.

Raney was the first African American nurse to report for duty in World War II and the first to be appointed chief nurse.
